carbon_policy_signal.item: EU greenhouse gas emissions rose in 2025, stagnating climate progress and weakening carbon abatement trajectory.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Rising emissions signal weaker ETS compliance and lower implicit carbon floor; pressures EUA downward (now 39th-percentile) and reduces carbon component of power marginal cost.

freshness_caveat: Coal data 175d stale (as of 2025-12-26); Clean Dark data 170d stale (as of 2025-12-31). Do not rely on coal-dark spread or historical comparisons on thermal merit order. Current clean spark and DE power signals are fresh.
risk_flags: Clean Spark 97th-percentile (60.96 EUR/MWh) — data 175d old on coal comps, but recent clean spark fresh; thermal regime extended.
EU Storage at 45.56% (21st-percentile), 14.3pp below seasonal — refill pace through summer critical to H2 power pricing.
TTF -21.79% monthly (40.52 EUR/MWh, 48th-percentile) — Permian LNG surge (27.6 Bcf/d) and rising US export capacity continue to weigh; watch arb closure if geopolitical risk reprices.
G7 Russia oil sanctions signal and potential Trump escalation could flip crude-LNG arb and TTF floor — tail-risk upside to gas if implemented.

base.summary: Gas softness persists on Permian LNG ramp; storage refill steady; thermal margins stable.

downside.summary: Permian LNG export ramp accelerates; US Gulf loading smooth; arb closure deepens; TTF declines on supply flood.

upside.summary: Trump escalates Russia oil sanctions; crude-LNG arb narrows; TTF reprices higher; storage lag compounds refill anxiety.

themes: Geopolitical risk premium on crude and LNG
top_drivers: Clean Spark at 97th-percentile (60.96 EUR/MWh) — gas weakness and high thermal spreads lift power; storage deficit compounds H2 refill urgency.
Permian LNG surge (27.6 Bcf/d) and rising US export capacity weaken TTF (-21.79% monthly) and compress power-gas spread; bearish near-term power via arb.
Geopolitical risk premium on crude (G7 Russia sanctions, Trump oil hints) supports LNG arb floor and creates tail-risk upside to gas if sanctions tightened; indirect power support.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 97th-percentile with gas weakness; storage 14.3pp below seasonal drives H2 thermal upside; geopolitical risk premium on oil supports LNG arb floor.

watchlist.why: At 21st-percentile and 14.3pp below seasonal; summer injection speed determines H2 thermal call intensity and power curve steepness.

watchlist.metric: Permian LNG export capacity and US Gulf loading
watchlist.why: 27.6 Bcf/d production surge pressuring TTF downside; any supply disruption (weather, maintenance) would narrow NW Europe arb and support gas.

watchlist.metric: Trump oil sanctions on Russia follow-through
watchlist.why: G7 reaffirmation plus Trump hints create tail-risk to crude-LNG arb and TTF price floor; direct 5–15% upside to gas if implemented this quarter.

watchlist.metric: EU emissions trend and EUA auction clearing
watchlist.why: Rising EU emissions in 2025 signal weaker carbon abatement; bearish for EUA (39th-percentile, -0.86% today) and carbon floor under power merit order.

watchlist_dated.name: European Council summit — trade, China, sanctions
watchlist_dated.why: Watch for Russia oil sanctions escalation signal and crude-LNG arb impact on TTF floor.
